text: Higher education research is a small field with imprecise boundaries between scholars closely affiliated to higher education research and scholars in other disciplines who occasionally engage with higher education research. Also, the boundaries are not always clear-cut between higher education researchers and other experts that are engaged in research on higher education through different channels and activities. Higher education research is a sizeable field of research in some European countries but a marginal one in others. The themes of higher education research are diverse and research is carried out within very different institutional settings in Europe. The national perspective is usually dominant. The distribution of higher education research findings across Europe is very uneven, reflecting the different roles played by the English language in the different European countries. International and European communication and cooperation among higher education researchers have developed strongly since the 1980s. Most studies in the field are comparative studies or are concerned with similar trends and developments in the European countries – either carried out by the researchers' own initiatives or as a consequence of the increasing importance of European-level policy and developments in higher education. The researchers see the European Commission's support to policy-oriented studies and 'targeted' research as either being of restrictive character restricting and obstructive, or as being potentially improving research and facilitating an appropriate dialogue on future higher education research and policy, as well as on the future development of higher education.
headline: Fully Human Written
